description_long: This is a traditional wooden mallet with a cylindrical handle and a large, rectangular block head. The head is made from a single piece of wood, showing visible grain patterns and some surface wear, indicating frequent use. The handle is straight, round, and tapers slightly towards the end, fitting securely into the head with a visible joint. The mallet's surface is matte and rough, with discoloration and scuff marks that suggest it has been used for striking or pounding tasks. There are no visible metal parts, and the entire object appears to be crafted from natural, unfinished wood. A faint marking or number is visible on one side of the head, but no brand or logo is present.
description: A well-worn wooden mallet with a rectangular block head and a straight cylindrical handle, showing signs of heavy use and natural wood grain.
description_view_0: The mallet is seen from an angled side view, highlighting the full length of the handle and the broad, flat face of the wooden head.
description_view_1: This top-down view shows the rectangular head's flat striking surface and the handle's attachment point at the center.
description_view_2: The opposite angled side view reveals the mallet's other face and the handle extending from the head, with visible wear on the wood.
description_view_3: A direct view along the handle axis, focusing on the end of the mallet's head and the straight alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth with visible grain, no ergonomic or grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular block wooden head, mallet type, with two flat striking faces and no claws or peens.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible surface wear, discoloration, scuff marks, and minor dents on the head and handle.
